vBRIEFInfo.description: Create UPGRADING.md, make QUICK-START.md the universal onboarding / upgrade funnel, and build the v0.19 -> v0.20 bridge (skill redirect stubs + template fallback rule + README banner).

plan.title: docs: create UPGRADING.md + make QUICK-START.md the universal onboarding funnel

narratives.Problem: Existing users updating their deft submodule to v0.20 have no reliable path to migration. Their project-root AGENTS.md (generated by the v0.19 installer) contains broken skill paths (`deft/skills/deft-sync/` etc.) that no longer exist. The agent hits a missing file and fails or improvises. QUICK-START.md already has a contributor/consumer gate and AGENTS.md copy/merge logic, but it has no upgrade detection and no mechanism to reach it from a stale AGENTS.md.
narratives.Action: Multi-surface v0.19 -> v0.20 bridge: (1) create 8 small deprecation-redirect SKILL.md stubs under skills/deft-{sync,setup,build,review-cycle,roadmap-refresh,swarm,pre-pr,interview}/ each pointing at deft/QUICK-START.md with a <!-- deft:deprecated-skill-redirect --> sentinel so stale v0.19 AGENTS.md paths don't dead-end; (2) add a template fallback rule to templates/agents-entry.md AND the mirror Go constant agentsMDEntry in cmd/deft-install/setup.go so any unreadable deft/skills/ path routes to QUICK-START.md on future version transitions; (3) rewrite QUICK-START.md with a new Step 2 (Detect project state) covering stale-AGENTS.md, pre-cutover artifacts (SPECIFICATION.md/PROJECT.md without the redirect sentinel), and partial migration, Step 3 Case F/G/H/I/J dispatch, and an explicit session-restart instruction after AGENTS.md rewrites or migrations; (4) create UPGRADING.md at the repo root as a versioned upgrade guide with a 'From any pre-v0.20 -> v0.20.0' section; (5) add a permanent README banner pointing at UPGRADING.md with an agent-directed `!` rule. Update 3 existing skill-naming tests to whitelist the 8 known redirect stub directories and add test_deprecated_skill_redirects.py for positive assertions on stub content, UPGRADING.md, README banner, QUICK-START.md detection, and the template / setup.go fallback rule.
narratives.Constraint: The 8 bare deft-* skill directories are intentionally NOT deft-directive-* -- they are deprecation stubs. Three existing naming tests (test_skills.py::test_no_bare_deft_skill_directories, test_vbrief_model.py::test_skills_dir_only_deft_directive_prefixed, test_skills_dir_has_no_bare_deft_prefix) require an explicit whitelist. The Go raw-string literal agentsMDEntry cannot contain backticks, so the template fallback rule uses plain `deft/skills/` / `deft/QUICK-START.md` without markdown code formatting so the test_template_matches_go_installer_constant string compare stays valid.
narratives.Test: tests/content/test_deprecated_skill_redirects.py -- 38 parametrised assertions covering: each of 8 stubs exists + has sentinel + points at QUICK-START.md + names replacement; no unexpected bare deft-* dirs; UPGRADING.md v0.20.0 / QUICK-START / BROWNFIELD / task migrate:vbrief / deft/run upgrade / new-session; README banner + agent rule; QUICK-START.md stale / pre-cutover / partial / session-restart / cross-refs; template + setup.go fallback rule.
narratives.Outcome: Any v0.19 user invoking QUICK-START.md is routed through explicit upgrade detection rather than falling into an undefined state. Stale AGENTS.md paths land on redirect stubs instead of 404s. Users browsing the repo see UPGRADING.md from the README banner. Future version transitions inherit the same fallback rule via the agents-entry template.

items.title: Add template fallback rule + sync setup.go agentsMDEntry
Rewrite QUICK-START.md with upgrade detection + session-restart
Add README upgrade banner with agent-directed rule
Whitelist 8 stubs in 3 existing skills-naming tests
